title_slide: Data Visualization & Storytelling with Python: Making Your Analysis Impossible to Ignore

slides.heading: The Problem with Raw Data
bullet_points: Numbers don't persuade — stories do
A story needs: problem → finding → action
Visualization is the language of data stories
slides.speaker_note: Open with the stakeholder non-response scenario — makes the problem feel immediately real before touching any code.

slides.heading: Matplotlib: The Foundation
bullet_points: Use fig, ax = plt.subplots() — not plt.plot() shortcuts
Axis objects give full control at any scale
Always export with dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight'
slides.speaker_note: Emphasize that Matplotlib's verbosity is a feature — it means you understand what you're building, not fighting it.

slides.heading: Seaborn: Statistical Defaults That Work
bullet_points: sns.set_style('whitegrid') removes chart junk
kde=True reveals distribution shape, not just bins
hue and size encode multiple dimensions cleanly
slides.speaker_note: Show the before/after: default Matplotlib colors vs. the Seaborn husl palette on identical data.

slides.heading: Choosing a Chart Is an Act of Framing
bullet_points: Line chart → implies trend or sequence
Bar chart → emphasizes categories and ranking
Scatter plot → reveals relationships and outliers
slides.speaker_note: Show the same dataset visualized three ways — same data, completely different impressions, none dishonest.

slides.heading: Mistake 1–3: Common Credibility Killers
bullet_points: Too many colors (6+ becomes unreadable — use gray)
Dual y-axes (honest subplots instead)
3D charts (heatmaps are faster and clearer)
slides.speaker_note: Show a bad example first, then the cleaned-up version — contrast lands harder than explanation alone.

slides.heading: Mistake 4: No Context
bullet_points: Wrong: 'Revenue Over Time'
Right: 'Revenue Grew 23% After Q3 Pricing Change'
Assume the reader sees this plot alone
slides.speaker_note: This is the mistake analysts make even after fixing everything else — title is written last but read first.

slides.heading: Visualization Is Communication, Not Decoration
bullet_points: Spend as much time on the plot as on the code
Remove every element that doesn't serve the story
Your insight doesn't matter until someone sees it
slides.speaker_note: End on the core reframe — this is the shift that changes how people approach their entire workflow.
